claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Thanks for helping me with this. I'm looking to claim for my child Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! You're very welcome, I'm happy to help you with your Child Benefit query for Alex.

To continue assessing your eligibility, I have a few more questions:

**Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you for confirming that Alex lives with you.

**Are you responsible for Alex's care and upbringing?** For example, do you pay for things like food, clothes, and other day-to-day needs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I look after Alex and pay for all the usual things - food, clothes, school stuff, all of that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely, thank you for that information.

**Are you and Alex living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, we both live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is Alex currently in full-time education or approved training?** (This question becomes more relevant as children get older, but I just want to confirm Alex's current situation at age 8.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex is just 8, so just in regular primary school - nothing special like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying! At age 8, attending primary school is completely normal and wouldn't affect your eligibility.

Just one more question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. Based on what you've told me, I have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me summarise your situation:
- Alex is 8 years old
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for Alex's care (paying for food, clothes, school items, etc.)
- No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

✅ **Age requirement met** – Alex is 8 years old (under 16)
✅ **Residency requirement met** – You and Alex both live in the UK
✅ **Responsibility requirement met** – Alex lives with you and you are responsible for their care, including paying for food, clothes, and other day-to-day needs
✅ **No conflicting claims** – No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You can apply for Child Benefit online or by post. If you have any other questions about the application process, feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, meeting the age requirement. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK and is responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, including paying for food, clothes, and school supplies. This meets the responsibility criteria under Child Benefit rules. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so there is no conflict with the rule that only one person can claim for a child. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant meets all the basic eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. They live in the UK with their child Alex (aged 8), they are responsible for Alex's care and upbringing, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. As Alex is under 16 and the claimant is clearly responsible for the child, they qualify for Child Benefit.

No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, so there is no conflict with the rule that only one person can claim for a child. All eligibility criteria are satisfied.

As Alex is under 16 and the claimant is clearly responsible for the child, they qualify for Child Benefit.
